system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|omega|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|omega|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    50;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        U               0.7;
        p               0.3;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo             0.5;

maxDeltaT         0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Current Error Analysis

Looking at the error log, the simulation is running but not completing within the allocated time/resources. The residuals are now very low (Ux ~1.7e-10, Uy ~4.2e-10, p ~8.6e-06), indicating the solution is converging well within each time step. However, the simulation is taking too long because:

1. **`nOuterCorrectors = 50`** — This means up to 50 PIMPLE outer iterations per time step, even though the solution converges in just 1-2 iterations (as shown by the residuals dropping to machine precision).
2. The time step is very small (`deltaT = 0.0001`), so the simulation needs many steps to reach `endTime = 0.3`.
3. With `adjustTimeStep yes` and `maxCo 0.5`, the adaptive time stepping may be further slowing progress.

The core issue is that **most outer iterations are wasted** because the solution is already converged (residuals at 10⁻¹⁰ level), yet PIMPLE continues iterating up to 50 times per time step.

## Root Cause

The `nOuterCorrectors = 50` setting causes excessive computation per time step. Since the residuals drop to convergence levels quickly, the PIMPLE loop should exit early, but with `nOuterCorrectors 50`, it still performs unnecessary iterations.

## Recommended Fix

### 1. Reduce `nOuterCorrectors` in `system/fvSolution`

Change from `50` to a more reasonable value like `10`:

```
nOuterCorrectors    10;
```

This caps the maximum work per time step while still allowing sufficient iterations for transient buildup. Given that residuals are already at 10⁻¹⁰ level, the solver will typically exit well before reaching 10 outer iterations anyway.

### 2. Enable Residual-Based Outer Loop Exit in `system/fvSolution`

Add residual monitoring to allow PIMPLE to exit early when converged. Modify the PIMPLE sub-dictionary in `fvSolution`:

```
PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    10;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;

    residualControl
    {
        U               1e-5;
        p               1e-5;
    }
}
```

The `residualControl` sub-dictionary allows PIMPLE to terminate outer iterations early once the residuals fall below the specified thresholds. With current residuals at ~10⁻⁹ to 10⁻¹⁰, the outer loop will exit after just 1-2 iterations instead of running all 10 (or 50) iterations.

### 3. Alternatively, if `residualControl` is not supported in this OpenFOAM version

Simply reducing `nOuterCorrectors` from 50 to 10 (or even 5) is sufficient. The residuals show the solution converges rapidly, so 5-10 outer correctors will be more than enough to capture the transient development while keeping computation per time step reasonable.

## Summary

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `system/fvSolution` | Reduce `nOuterCorrectors` from 50 to **10**; optionally add `residualControl { U 1e-5; p 1e-5; }` to enable early termination of outer loops |

This is the key fix: the simulation is computationally expensive because it performs up to 50 unnecessary outer iterations per time step when the solution is already fully converged. Limiting outer correctors to 10 (and enabling residual-based early exit) will allow the simulation to progress through time steps efficiently and reach the `endTime` of 0.3 within available resources.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSolution', 'changes': 'Reduce nOuterCorrectors from 50 to 10; Add residualControl sub-dictionary inside PIMPLE block with U 1e-5 and p 1e-5 to enable early termination of outer iterations when converged'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 10m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-5.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
